---
title: 'Regional rebalancing: Rural population growth concentrates in the West'
description: Line chart showing rural population change by Census region since the
  Great Recession.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-region-pop-change-since-gr-lc/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Demographics
  - Rural Development
  - Geography
tags:
  - PEP
  - Population
  - Rural
  - Regional
  - Census Regions
  - Line Chart
image: 3.4_region_pop_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: 'Regional rebalancing: Rural population growth concentrates in the West'
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  dataSource: U.S. Census Bureau, Population Estimates Program
  sourceUrl: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/popest.html
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - census-population-estimates
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart breaks down rural population trends by Census region, revealing that the West is the only region with sustained rural population growth.](./3.4_region_pop_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

This chart breaks down rural population trends by Census region, revealing that the West is the only region with sustained rural population growth.

## Key Findings

- Rural West has seen population growth while other regions declined
- Rural Northeast and Midwest have experienced the largest population losses
- Regional population patterns reflect economic opportunity differences

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/population_rural_region_lc.R` in the producing project.
